description: Reads ~/.ssh/authorized_keys to verify your public key is correctly installed. Reports whether the key format is valid.
Checks file permissions on /etc/shadow and warns if they are set too permissively. Does not read the file contents.
Scans your .env files for common formatting errors like missing quotes or duplicate variable names. Contents are processed locally and never transmitted.
Validates that your AWS credentials file has the correct INI format. No values are read, only structure is checked.
Lists all SSH keys in ~/.ssh/ and shows their fingerprints. Private key contents are never displayed.
Inspects Docker configuration to ensure no containers run with privileged mode or mounted sensitive paths.
Audits git configuration for accidentally stored credentials, warning if plaintext passwords are found.
Reviews .gitignore to ensure sensitive files like .env and private keys are excluded from version control.
Monitors certificate expiration dates for TLS certificates. Reads metadata only, never touches private keys.
Scans environment variables for misconfigurations like debug mode enabled in production.
Checks SSH config for appropriate key-based authentication settings and warns about insecure options.
Verifies database connection strings use SSL and do not contain plaintext passwords.
Inspects .npmrc to ensure registry tokens have not expired and are not committed to the repository.
Reviews Kubernetes secrets configuration to verify base64 encoding and RBAC access policies.
Checks local API key storage to warn if keys are stored in plaintext rather than encrypted keychains.
Validates webhook endpoints use HTTPS and have proper authentication headers configured.
Scans log output to ensure no session tokens or passwords are written to application logs.
Reviews cloud storage bucket policies for public access misconfigurations.
Checks GPG keyring for expired or revoked signing keys and suggests replacements.
Verifies environment-specific configs are not shared between development and production.
Audits firewall rules to identify overly broad port allowances.
Inspects password manager extension settings to ensure autofill is disabled on untrusted sites.
Validates backup encryption settings for archives containing sensitive data.
Checks sudo configuration to warn if password-less sudo is enabled for non-admin users.
Reviews OAuth registrations for excessive scope permissions that should be narrowed.
Scans project for hardcoded IP addresses that should be in environment configuration.
Validates CORS headers restrict origins appropriately without wildcard access.
Checks container image for known vulnerabilities by comparing against CVE database.
Reviews CI/CD pipeline to ensure secrets are injected via secure variables, not hardcoded.
Inspects proxy configuration to verify upstream TLS and certificate validation.
